overview.short: Pandavgad Fort) (also called Pandugad) (Wai T; 17° 55' N, 73° 50' E; RS.
overview.description: Pandavgad Fort) (also called Pandugad) (Wai T; 17° 55' N, 73° 50' E; RS. Wathar, 20 m. SE;), 4177 feet above sea level, lies four miles north-west of Wai. The fort is conspicuous over a low spur branching east from its southern angle. From a distance it appears a square fortification with natural escarpment of cut black stone.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Wai Caves
topPlaces.description: Wai Caves are 9 Buddhist Caves, situated at Lonara, 7 km north of Wai. The Chaitya hall containing a Stupa has since been converted into a Shiva temple.

topPlaces.name: Pasarni
topPlaces.description: Pasarni is a village in the taluka of Wai in the Satara district of Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Wai, Maharashtra
topPlaces.description: Wai (ISO: Vāī; Pronunciation: [ʋaːi] ) is a town in Satara district of Maharashtra state in India. Located on the Krishna River, Wai was a prominent town during the Peshwa era.

topPlaces.name: Dhom Dam
topPlaces.description: Dhom Dam is an earthfill and gravity dam on Krishna River near Wai in state of Maharashtra in India.

items.note: Wai Caves are 9 Buddhist Caves, situated at Lonara, 7 km north of Wai. The Chaitya hall containing a Stupa has since been converted into a Shiva temple.
Pasarni is a village in the taluka of Wai in the Satara district of Maharashtra.
Wai (ISO: Vāī; Pronunciation: [ʋaːi] ) is a town in Satara district of Maharashtra state in India. Located on the Krishna River, Wai was a prominent town during the Peshwa era.
Dhom Dam is an earthfill and gravity dam on Krishna River near Wai in state of Maharashtra in India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pandavgad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pandavgad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Pune Airport (65 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (59 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pandavgad?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pandavgad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pandavgad?
faq.a: Stays in Pandavgad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Pandavgad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Wai Caves, Pasarni, Wai, Maharashtra, Dhom Dam.
